stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The context is related to text-generation capabilities within the AI ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core area of text generation using transformer-based models.
The mention of Llama and Hugging Face indicates relevance within the current generative AI ecosystem.

stories.title: Microsoft's Project Solara is an Android OS designed for agents instead of apps
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Microsoft is developing 'Project Solara,' a new Android OS.
The OS is specifically designed to run AI 'agents' rather than traditional mobile applications.
This signals a potential industry shift away from app-centric mobile experiences towards agent-driven interaction.

stories.title: Amazon-owned Ring should pay Americans for scanning their faces, lawsuit says
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Ring cameras, owned by Amazon, are allegedly using AI to scan and identify faces of guests and passersby.
A lawsuit claims that Ring should compensate Americans for this facial scanning technology.
The story highlights significant privacy concerns regarding the deployment of AI surveillance in consumer smart home devices.

stories.title: Humanoid-GPT: Scaling Data and Structure for Zero-Shot Motion Tracking
stories.author: Li Yi
bullet_points: Introduction of Humanoid-GPT, a GPT-style Transformer designed for whole-body motion tracking in humanoids.
The model is trained on a massive, unified 2B-frame motion corpus, addressing limitations of prior shallow MLP trackers.
Scaling data and model capacity allows Humanoid-GPT to track highly dynamic behaviors with improved generalization.

stories.title: Gemma 4 12B: A unified, encoder-free multimodal model
stories.author: rvz
bullet_points: Introduction of 'Gemma 4 12B,' a new large language model.
The model is highlighted as a unified, encoder-free multimodal architecture.
The announcement originates from Hacker News, suggesting interest within the technical community.

stories.title: Full Disclosure: 1-Click GitHub Token Stealing via a VSCode Bug
stories.author: blog.ammaraskar.com by ammar2
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability exists in VSCode that allows for one-click GitHub token theft.
The exploit enables attackers to steal GitHub tokens with minimal user interaction.
This represents a significant security risk within the developer ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical LLM deployment.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language models (LLMs).
Key associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, pointing to standard ML infrastructure.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating an ongoing optimization effort for local AI on Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is running AI functionality entirely on the user's own device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users via a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Nvidia is already planning N2X and N3X chips — the goal is the Star Trek computer
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Nvidia is planning future chip generations (N2X and N3X) beyond the initial RTX Spark.
The development of these chips is framed with an ambitious, high-performance goal ('Star Trek computer').
CEO Jensen Huang confirmed the multi-generational roadmap for RTX Spark at Computex 2026.

stories.title: As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise
stories.author: TC. Sottek
bullet_points: Google's new Gemini AI agent, Spark, is being reviewed in depth.
The agent demonstrates highly advanced capabilities, exhibiting surprising levels of personal knowledge.
The review suggests the AI's effectiveness is both impressive and potentially concerning ('scary').

stories.title: Amazon’s search bar will invent AI-generated products you can’t buy
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon is integrating an AI-powered search feature that generates product images based on user descriptions.
Currently, the feature is limited to generating AI images for clothing and home goods.
The AI-generated images act as visual aids, allowing users to find similar purchasable items.

stories.title: Android phones will soon be able to detect spoofed calls and impersonation scams
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Android is receiving a new feature drop that enhances scam detection capabilities.
The update specifically targets the detection of spoofed calls and impersonation scams.
The release bundles this security feature alongside updates to AirDrop and general AI enhancements.

stories.title: Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering for Efficient and Controllable LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Julian McAuley
bullet_points: Introduces Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering (ACTS) to improve LLM reasoning efficiency and controllability.
Addresses the inefficiency and lack of inference-time control in standard extended chain-of-thought reasoning.
Formulates reasoning steering as a Markov decision process managed by a controller agent.

stories.title: VLESA: Vision-Language Embodied Safety Agent for Human Activity Monitoring
stories.author: Changliu Liu
bullet_points: Introduces VLESA, a Vision-Language Embodied Safety Agent designed for monitoring human activities.
The framework uses egocentric video to predict and trigger real-time safety interventions for dangerous actions.
It specifically addresses 'intent-dependent safety,' where the safety of an action depends on the user's goal.

stories.title: Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering for Efficient and Controllable LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Julian McAuley
bullet_points: Introduces Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering (ACTS) to improve LLM reasoning efficiency and controllability.
Addresses the inefficiency and lack of inference-time control in standard extended chain-of-thought methods.
Formulates reasoning steering as a Markov decision process managed by a controller agent.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: The best Qi2 batteries for iPhone and Pixel
stories.author: Cameron Faulkner
bullet_points: New Qi2.2-certified power banks offer significant speed improvements.
These compact batteries can wirelessly charge newer iPhones (iPhone 16+) at up to 25W.
The integration of MagSafe allows for magnetic attachment and convenience.

stories.title: PlayStation is getting back to what it’s good at
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: PlayStation is shifting its development focus away from live-service games.
The console manufacturer is prioritizing premium, narrative-driven, single-player titles.
This strategic pivot was highlighted during the recent State of Play showcase.

stories.title: Microsoft and OpenAI broke up — now they’re ready to fight
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is aggressively expanding its AI portfolio at its Build conference.
New initiatives include a super app, in-house reasoning models, and AI agents.
The announcement signals Microsoft's intent to be a major, independent player in the AI landscape.

stories.title: Microsoft plans Linux tools and an RTX Spark desktop for Windows developers
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Microsoft is expanding its support for Linux tools within the Windows ecosystem.
The announcement includes plans for a new RTX Spark desktop aimed at Windows developers.
The news highlights several software developments showcased at Microsoft Build.

stories.title: Mathematicians warn of AI threats to profession as industry encroaches
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Mathematicians have issued warnings regarding the growing influence of the tech industry on the field of mathematics.
The International Mathematical Union (IMU) has officially endorsed these concerns about industry encroachment.
The article discusses the potential threats AI and related technologies pose to the mathematical profession.

stories.title: Imaginative Perception Tokens Enhance Spatial Reasoning in Multimodal Language Models
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language Models (VLMs): poor spatial reasoning when information is not directly visible.
A novel mechanism, Imaginative Perception Tokens (IPT), is introduced to externalize and represent what a VLM would perceive from unseen viewpoints or occluded spaces.
IPT aims to enhance the ability of VLMs to perform complex spatial reasoning tasks by facilitating 'imaginative perception'.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories
stories.author: Vahab Mirrokni
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in current LLMs: the inability to continually learn and transfer temporal in-context knowledge to long-term parameters.
The proposed method is inspired by human learning processes, specifically focusing on the concept of 'sleep' for memory consolidation.
The work aims to enable LLMs to self-modify and improve their long-term memory capabilities, moving beyond instant prediction tasks.

stories.title: Quantifying Faithful Confidence Expression in Large Reasoning Models
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical issue of 'faithful calibration' (FC) in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs).
FC refers to the alignment between a model's actual uncertainty and the confidence it linguistically expresses.
This research is important because users often interpret LRM reasoning traces as evidence of competence and confidence.

stories.title: QUBRIC: Co-Designing Queries and Rubrics for RL Beyond Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Chao Zhang
bullet_points: QUBRIC addresses a limitation in rubric-based Reinforcement Learning (RL) where rubric quality is constrained by fixed query structures.
The framework tackles the issue where open-ended queries lead to vague rubrics, and narrowing them creates unverifiable, fabricated references.
QUBRIC introduces a co-design approach to simultaneously optimize queries and rubrics, aiming to extend RL beyond verifiable reward signals.

stories.title: AlignAtt4LLM: Fast AlignAtt for Decoder-Only LLMs at IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation Task
stories.author: Dominik Macháček
bullet_points: Introduces AlignAtt4LLM, a new system for simultaneous speech translation (English to German, Italian, Chinese) targeting IWSLT 2026.
The system uses a synchronous cascade, combining Qwen3-ASR for incremental transcription and Gemma-4 E4B-it for translation.
It marks a novel application of AlignAtt to decoder-only LLMs, adapting the technique without traditional encoder-decoder cross-attention.

stories.title: Self-Refining Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Vision-Conditioned UAV Navigation
stories.author: Dzmitry Tsetserukou
bullet_points: Introduces AgenticRL, a framework designed to enhance autonomy in reinforcement learning for robotics.
Addresses the limitation of traditional RL methods which heavily rely on time-consuming, human-designed reward functions.
Focuses on applying this self-refining agentic learning to complex navigation tasks for UAVs (drones).

stories.title: Using Reward Uncertainty to Induce Diverse Behaviour in Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Mark Rowland
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in classical Reinforcement Learning (RL) where policies tend to be deterministic, which is insufficient for modern tasks like language model fine-tuning.
Existing methods for inducing diversity (e.g., entropy regularization) often involve fragile trade-offs or rely on potentially misleading heuristic metrics.
The proposed solution leverages 'Reward Uncertainty' as a more natural mechanism to encourage diverse behavior within RL frameworks.

stories.title: Neuron Populations Exhibit Divergent Selectivity with Scale
stories.author: Yossi Gandelsman
bullet_points: The research investigates how neuron population selectivity changes as the scale of neural networks increases.
It extends scaling laws beyond traditional metrics like model loss by examining internal network properties.
The study uses Rosetta Neurons and analyzes large language (up to 30B) and vision models (up to 5B).

stories.title: Skill-RM: Unifying Heterogeneous Evaluation Criteria via Agent Skill
stories.author: Guanjun Jiang
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of integrating heterogeneous evaluation criteria (rule-based, ground-truth, rubrics) in LLM post-training.
Introduces Skill Reward Model (Skill-RM) as a unified framework for reward model evaluation.
Focuses on improving Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RFT) and Reinforcement Learning (RL) pipelines for LLMs.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories
stories.author: Vahab Mirrokni
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of current LLMs: the inability to continually learn and transfer temporal in-context knowledge to long-term parameters.
Proposes a novel approach inspired by human learning, suggesting LLMs require a mechanism akin to 'sleep' for memory consolidation.
Focuses on enabling self-modification within language models to improve long-term learning capabilities.

stories.title: Using Reward Uncertainty to Induce Diverse Behaviour in Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Mark Rowland
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in classical Reinforcement Learning (RL) where policies tend to be deterministic, failing to capture the need for diverse behavior in modern applications.
It proposes using 'Reward Uncertainty' as a novel mechanism to naturally induce diversity in RL, moving beyond existing methods like entropy regularization.
The research aims to overcome the performance-sacrificing trade-offs and heuristic misalignment issues found in current diversity-inducing RL techniques.

stories.title: MLSkip: Data Skipping for ML Filters via Lightweight Metadata
stories.author: Andreas Kipf
bullet_points: New AI functions in database filters rely on costly, black-box ML models, creating data management challenges.
Traditional data skipping techniques are ineffective for these new ML-based filter predicates.
The research proposes studying novel data skipping mechanisms to efficiently prune non-qualifying row groups.

stories.title: SEAOTTER: Sensor Embedded Autoencoding with One-Time Transcode for Efficient Reconstruction
stories.author: Neeraja J. Yadwadkar
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of processing high-resolution visual data in resource-constrained robotics systems.
It proposes SEAOTTER, a novel approach using Sensor Embedded Autoencoding with One-Time Transcode for efficient data reconstruction.
The method aims to overcome bandwidth and on-device compute limitations associated with conventional and newer video codecs.

stories.title: FlashbackCL: Mitigating Temporal Forgetting in Federated Learning
stories.author: Horacio Gonzalez-Velez
bullet_points: Addresses temporal forgetting in Federated Learning (FL) when client data distributions drift over time.
Identifies a limitation in existing FL forgetting mitigation methods, which assume stationary client distributions.
Proposes 'FlashbackCL' to address this, as the knowledge proxy used in current methods becomes miscalibrated under temporal shift.

stories.title: q0: Primitives for Hyper-Epoch Pretraining
stories.author: Samip Dahal
bullet_points: Addresses the inefficiency of single-model pretraining when compute budgets are large.
Proposes 'hyper-epoch pretraining' (q0) as a conceptual shift from single models to model populations.
Aims to utilize multi-epoch compute budgets by aggregating predictions from diverse models.

stories.title: Neuron Populations Exhibit Divergent Selectivity with Scale
stories.author: Yossi Gandelsman
bullet_points: The research investigates how neuron populations in neural networks change as the model scale increases, moving beyond traditional metrics like loss.
The study utilizes 'Rosetta Neurons,' a specific class of neurons whose activation patterns are consistent across different independently trained models.
Observations were made on both large language models (up to 30B parameters) and vision models (up to 5B parameters).

stories.title: Skill-RM: Unifying Heterogeneous Evaluation Criteria via Agent Skill
stories.author: Guanjun Jiang
bullet_points: Skill-RM proposes a unified framework to address the challenge of integrating heterogeneous evaluation criteria for LLM reward models.
Current LLM post-training (like RFT/RL) relies on diverse feedback signals (rule-based, ground-truth, rubrics) that lack a unified integration mechanism.
The framework aims to improve the reliability and comprehensiveness of reward signals used in large language model fine-tuning.

stories.title: Quantifying Faithful Confidence Expression in Large Reasoning Models
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical issue of 'faithful calibration' (FC) in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs).
FC refers to the alignment between a model's actual uncertainty and the confidence it linguistically expresses.
This research is vital for improving the trustworthiness of LLMs, especially when their reasoning traces are interpreted by users.

stories.title: QUBRIC: Co-Designing Queries and Rubrics for RL Beyond Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Chao Zhang
bullet_points: QUBRIC addresses a limitation in rubric-based Reinforcement Learning (RL) where rubric quality is constrained by fixed query structures.
The framework tackles the issue of open-ended queries leading to vague rubrics or overly constrained queries causing training failure.
QUBRIC introduces a novel approach to co-designing queries and rubrics to enable RL beyond reliance on strictly verifiable rewards.

stories.title: AlignAtt4LLM: Fast AlignAtt for Decoder-Only LLMs at IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation Task
stories.author: Dominik Macháček
bullet_points: Introduces AlignAtt4LLM, a new system for simultaneous speech translation (English to German, Italian, Chinese) for the IWSLT 2026 task.
The system uses a synchronous cascade combining Qwen3-ASR for transcription and Gemma-4 E4B-it for translation.
It marks a novel application of AlignAtt to decoder-only LLMs, adapting the technique without traditional encoder-decoder cross-attention.

stories.title: Efficient ASR Training with Conversations that Never Happened
stories.author: Péter Mihajlik
bullet_points: Addresses the data scarcity problem in Conversational Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) for low-resource languages.
Proposes an augmentation pipeline that synthesizes realistic, scenario-level dialogues using LLMs.
Evaluates various LLM families to generate speaker-aware simulated conversations for ASR training.

stories.title: Value-Aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Robin Jia
bullet_points: Addresses the memory and compute bottleneck in large reasoning models caused by long outputs.
Proposes a 'Value-Aware Stochastic KV Cache Eviction' method to manage cache size.
Identifies that evicting certain value states with abnormally large magnitudes is a key factor for improving eviction accuracy.

stories.title: Run AI Coding Agents Safely with Docker Sandboxes
stories.author: pradumnasaraf
bullet_points: The article addresses the security risks associated with running AI coding agents.
It proposes using Docker sandboxes as a method to safely execute untrusted AI agent commands.
The core problem is preventing AI agents from making unauthorized file modifications or downloads.
